commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Contributor and human follow-up are needed because the PR requires real behavior proof plus conflict-resolution/code changes that preserve existing WhatsApp behavior and avoid sensitive logging.; Needs attention: The diff does not change dependencies or workflows, but it introduces logging of list-reply titles and row ids that should be redacted before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve upload-file when adding li... (sha=c57f4546689146042627166ab8925a8c357d4d35)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds WhatsApp interactive list extraction, untrusted structured list context, a `list-reply` message action, docs, and focused WhatsApp/message-tool tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking PR defects: source inspection shows current main supports WhatsApp `upload-file` and plugin-owned action aliases, while the PR head drops `upload-file` routing and hardcodes WhatsApp aliases in core. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor and human follow-up are needed because the PR requires real behavior proof plus conflict-resolution/code changes that preserve existing WhatsApp behavior and avoid sensitive logging.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): support list reply actions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp interactive list prompts expose stable row ids, but agents could not see or select them reliably.
- Why it matters: business and service flows often require a real WhatsApp `singleSelectReply.selectedRowId`, not a plain-text answer.
- What changed: extract inbound list rows as readable text and untrusted structured context, count list prompts as inbound content, add `list-reply`, preserve quote metadata, and cover the path with focused tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol change, no auth or credential behavior change, and no config schema change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83217
- Related #73823
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: WhatsApp agents can inspect inbound interactive list rows and select a row with `message` action `list-reply` using the captured `selectedRowId` and visible row `title`.
- Real environment tested: Local automated test suite plus a live linked WhatsApp Web session against real WhatsApp Business list flows, including Meta's public Jasper's Market sample flow.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`\n  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`\n  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`\n  - `pnpm check:changed`
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
  - `pnpm build`
- Evidence after fix: Unit/seam tests passed locally; live session logs show real inbound business list prompts rendered with `Options`/`rowId`, successful native `message(action="list-reply")` calls returning `ok: true`, and the flow advancing after selected list rows. The follow-up schema guard verifies list-only fields are visible but not required for ordinary message actions.
- Observed result after fix: The real business flows could be driven through list-backed choices on the linked WhatsApp session; the Jasper sample prompt exposed row ids such as `reply-interactive-with-media`, `reply-media-card-carousel`, and `reply-offer`, and `list-reply` selection returned successfully.
- What was not tested: A broad matrix of other WhatsApp Business providers, group chats, and every native-flow button variant beyond the covered list/button shapes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #83217 documents the missing current-main support and includes example WhatsApp list screenshots.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A. This is a new WhatsApp feature request rather than a regression in existing behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts`, `src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: direct and wrapped list prompt extraction, structured context propagation, content gating, canonical message action schema, row id/title validation, outbound payload shape, and quote metadata preservation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior spans plugin extraction, action discovery/dispatch, and the Baileys send seam without needing a live WhatsApp account for deterministic payload proof.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A before this change.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

WhatsApp now advertises and supports a native `list-reply` message action for inbound list prompts. Agents should pass `selectedRowId` and the visible row `title`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable text only / no row-id action -> agent sends plain text or stalls

After:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable options + untrusted structured context -> list-reply action -> WhatsApp singleSelectReply.selectedRowId
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This adds a WhatsApp `list-reply` message action under the existing message tool. It is gated by the existing WhatsApp `actions.sendMessage` gate plus account/allowlist authorization, requires an explicit `selectedRowId` and visible `title`, treats inbound list context as untrusted, and does not expose secrets or raw Baileys payloads to agents.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local developer environment (redacted)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): WhatsApp channel test configs and mocked active listener/send APIs

### Steps

1. Run the focused WhatsApp/action tests listed in Real behavior proof.
2. Run `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`.
3. Run `pnpm check:changed`.
4. Run `pnpm build`.

### Expected

- Tests pass, changed checks pass, build passes, and no whitespace errors are reported.

### Actual

- All listed commands exited 0.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: inbound list extraction including native-flow and button-backed prompts, list response text rendering, message action discovery/schema, action dispatch, outbound list/button reply payload shapes, quote metadata preservation, SDK export guardrails, docs entry, and redacted live WhatsApp Business sample flow logs.
- Edge cases checked: missing selected row id, missing title, missing target, disabled `sendMessage` action, LID-backed quote metadata, unsupported camelCase alias, wrapped list messages, native-flow list prompts, button-backed prompts, synthetic button/template/native-flow response text.
- What you did **not** verify: a broad matrix of other WhatsApp Business providers, group chats, and every native-flow button variant beyond the covered list/button shapes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Live WhatsApp Business list flows may require provider-specific nuance not captured by mocked Baileys tests.
  - Mitigation: The implementation uses Baileys' native `listReply` payload shape, preserves quote metadata, keeps the action behind existing send gating, and includes focused seam tests around payload construction.
